rules.title: Imperative Formation - Basic Structure
rules.text: Slovak imperative is used for commands, requests, and instructions. It has three forms: 2nd person singular (ty), 1st person plural (my - 'let's'), and 2nd person plural/formal (vy). The imperative is formed from the present tense stem with specific endings.

rules.title: Imperative Endings - Regular Pattern
rules.text: Most verbs follow this pattern: Remove present tense ending, add imperative endings. Singular (ty): no ending or -Ø, 1st plural (my): -me, 2nd plural (vy): -te.

rules.title: Imperative Conjugation - 'robiť' (to do)
rules.text: Full imperative conjugation for 'robiť'. Based on present tense stem 'rob-'.
examples: Robme to spolu! – Let's do it together!
Robte to rýchlo! – Do it quickly! (formal/plural)

rules.title: Imperative Conjugation - 'písať' (to write)
rules.text: Imperative for 'písať'. Note the consonant change š in the stem.
examples: Píšme list! – Let's write a letter!

rules.title: Imperative Conjugation - 'čítať' (to read)
rules.text: Imperative for 'čítať'. Based on present stem 'číta-'.

rules.title: Imperative with -aj Ending
rules.text: Verbs with present tense ending in -a/-á add -j in singular imperative, -jme in 1st plural, -jte in 2nd plural.

rules.title: Imperative with Consonant Stem
rules.text: Verbs with consonant-ending stems in present tense typically have no ending in singular imperative.

rules.title: Perfective vs Imperfective in Imperative
rules.text: Perfective imperatives command a single completed action. Imperfective imperatives command ongoing, repeated, or general actions.
examples: Píš mi často! – Write to me often! (repeated action)
Napíš mi zajtra! – Write to me tomorrow! (single action)
Čítaj každý deň! – Read every day! (habitual)
Prečítaj túto knihu! – Read this book through! (complete)

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Imperative - Word Order
rules.text: Reflexive particle (sa/si) comes AFTER the imperative verb form. Word order: Imperative + sa/si.
examples: Obliekaj sa rýchlo! – Get dressed quickly!
Učme sa spolu! – Let's learn together!

rules.title: Reflexive Imperative - Perfective Forms
rules.text: Perfective reflexive imperatives: verb + sa/si for completed actions.
examples: Nauč sa to! – Learn it! (master it)
Naučme sa slovenčinu! – Let's learn Slovak!

rules.title: Negation in Imperative
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the imperative form. Reflexive particle stays after the verb. Negative imperatives often express prohibitions.
examples: Nepíš mi! – Don't write to me!
Nerobme to! – Let's not do it!

rules.title: Negation with Reflexives
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to imperative. Reflexive particle stays after the verb: ne-imperative + sa/si.
examples: Neuč sa to! – Don't learn that!
Neučme sa to! – Let's not learn that!

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'byť' (to be)
rules.text: The verb 'byť' has irregular imperative forms.

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'mať' (to have)
rules.text: The verb 'mať' has irregular imperative forms.
examples: Maj sa! – Take care! (literally: Have yourself!)

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'ísť' (to go)
rules.text: The verb 'ísť' has irregular imperative forms based on 'choď-'.

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'prísť' (to come)
rules.text: The verb 'prísť' has irregular imperative forms based on 'poď-'.
examples: Poď so mnou! – Come with me!

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'jesť' (to eat)
rules.text: The verb 'jesť' has irregular imperative forms based on 'jedz-'.

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'piť' (to drink)
rules.text: The verb 'piť' has irregular imperative forms based on 'pi-'.

rules.title: Irregular Imperative - 'vedieť' (to know)
rules.text: The verb 'vedieť' has irregular imperative forms based on 'vedz-'.
examples: Vedzme pravdu! – Let's know the truth!

rules.title: Common Irregular Imperatives
rules.text: Summary of the most common irregular imperative forms that must be memorized.
examples: Daj mi to! – Give it to me!

rules.title: Polite Requests with Imperative
rules.text: Add 'prosím' (please) or 'prosím ťa/vás' to make imperatives more polite. Can come before or after the imperative.
examples: Prosím, rob to! – Please, do it!
Rob to, prosím! – Do it, please!
Prosím ťa, pomôž mi! – Please help me! (informal)
Poď sem, prosím! – Come here, please!

rules.title: Softening Imperatives
rules.text: Use modal particles or conditional forms to soften commands. 'Môžeš' (can you) or conditional makes requests less direct.
examples: Môžeš mi pomôcť? – Can you help me? (softer than 'Pomôž mi!')
Mohol by si mi pomôcť? – Could you help me? (conditional, very polite)
Pomohol by si mi? – Would you help me?
Prosím ťa, môžeš to urobiť? – Please, can you do it?
